channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Gaza ceasefire talks press on in Qatar
item.description: Israel and Hamas appear to be inching closer to a phased ceasfire agreement, officials said Monday. However, a deal hasn't been reached yet and there are a number of obstacles that could still scupper the talks in Qatar. The war in Gaza has ground on for more than 15 months, starting on Oct.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel, Hamas ceasefire close, but not 100 percent: Israeli official
item.description: Efforts to secure a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas and release hostages are close but not yet finalized, an Israeli official told The Hill on Monday, after Reuters reported Qatar has handed a final deal to both sides.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Magnitude 6.9 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan as tsunami threat declared over
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A strong earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 6.9 hit southwestern Japan on Monday, triggering public warnings to stay away from coastal areas because of a tsunami advisory, which was later called off. There were no immediate reports of damage.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russian forces bypass a key stronghold in a bid to cut off its supplies, a Ukrainian officer says
item.description: A Ukrainian official says Russian forces are bypassing a key stronghold in eastern Ukraine that they have fought for months to capture and are focusing instead on cutting supply lines to it.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab And Yehor Konovalov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Seoul's spy agency says North Korean soldiers captured in Ukraine haven't shown desire to defect
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s spy agency told lawmakers on Monday that two North Korean soldiers who were captured by Ukrainian forces while fighting alongside Russian forces in Russia’s Kursk border region haven’t expressed a desire to seek asylum in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: India kicks off a massive Hindu festival touted as the world's largest religious gathering
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Millions of Hindu devotees, mystics and holy men and women from all across India flocked to the northern city of Prayagraj on Monday to kickstart the Maha Kumbh festival, which is being touted as the world's largest religious gathering.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italian photographer Oliviero Toscani, famed for provocative Benetton campaigns, dies at 82
item.description: Oliviero Toscani, the photographer behind Benetton’s provocative ad campaigns of the 1980s and 1990s who later broke with the Italian knitwear brand under controversy, has died at age 82.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: Officials say U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal has not been reached yet.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Eastwood, Samy Magdy And Josef Federman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the attempts to arrest impeached South Korean President Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — For weeks, impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has holed up inside his guarded residential compound as investigators try to figure out how to detain him. Scuffles occurred Jan.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Croatia's President Milanović wins another term after defeating ruling party candidate in runoff
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia's opposition-backed President Zoran Milanović, a critic of the European Union and NATO, overwhelmingly won reelection for another five-year term on Sunday, defeating a candidate from the ruling conservative party in a runoff vote, official results showed.
item.dc:creator: SAŠA KAVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 poll finds majority of Greenland respondents support joining US
item.description: A new survey found that a majority of Greenland respondents support joining the United States. According to a poll by Patriot Polling released Sunday, 57.3 percent of respondents approve of Greenland becoming part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine has captured 2 North Korean soldiers, South Korea's intelligence service says
item.description: Ukraine captured two wounded North Korean soldiers who were fighting on behalf of Russia in a Russian border region, South Korea’s intelligence service said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspended South Korean President Yoon to skip first day of impeachment trial over safety concerns
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol will not attend the first day of his impeachment trial this week, according to his lawyer, who cited concerns for the suspended president’s safety.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Mayotte on red alert again as another cyclone heads toward the French territory off Africa
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The French territory of Mayotte was put on red alert Saturday as another cyclone headed toward the islands off Africa that were devastated by their worst storm in nearly a century last month. The Prefecture of Mayotte said the red alert warning was issued at 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trams collide in the French city of Strasbourg, injuring dozens, though none critically
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Two trams collided in Strasbourg in eastern France on Saturday, causing dozens of injuries, though none critical, authorities said. The accident took place during the afternoon in a tunnel leading to the station near the city's central train station.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says it will continue oil and gas projects despite US sanctions
item.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry has denounced U.S. sanctions against Moscow's energy sector and said the country would carry on with large oil and gas projects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian intelligence agency says it thwarted a planned Islamic State attack on a Shiite shrine
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Intelligence officials in Syria's new de facto government thwarted a plan by the Islamic State group to set off a bomb at a Shiite shrine in the Damascus suburb of Sayyida Zeinab, state media reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sudan’s military retakes a strategic city from RSF rebels, dealing them a major blow
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan’s military and its allies have taken back a strategic city from the rebel Rapid Support Forces, officials said Saturday, dealing a major blow to the notorious paramilitary group which the United States accused of committing genocide in the country’s ongoing civil war.
item.dc:creator: BY SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One of four lynx captured in the Scottish Highlands dies
item.description: LONDON (AP) — One of four lynx thought to have been released illegally in the Scottish Highlands has died within hours after it was captured, wildlife authorities said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black boxes from South Korea plane crash failed to record final 4 minutes, officials say
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The black boxes of a Boeing jetliner that crashed in South Korea last month stopped recording about four minutes before the accident, South Korean officials said Saturday, possibly complicating investigations into the cause of the disaster that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden admin slammed for ‘waiting’ to declare genocide in Sudan
item.description: The incoming Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee has blasted the Biden Admnin for waiting until just 13 days before their term ends to declare atrocities in Sudan’s war as genocide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine doubles down on Kursk ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Ukraine is leaning into its efforts to hold onto the Russian territory of Kursk amid intense pressure from Russian and North Korean forces to take it back, apparently gambling that the region could be a valuable card in potential negotiations with Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lebanon's new president strikes a nationalistic tone amid regional shifts, further weakening of Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese parliament finally voted on a new president on Thursday following two years of deadlock. Joseph Aoun the head of the country's armed forces won a majority of the votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Venezuelan opposition supporters take to the streets ahead of Maduro's third inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition supporters protested around the country in a last-minute effort to put pressure on President Maduro the day before he is to be sworn in for his third term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel sends report to UN on 'brutal' treatment used by terrorists against hostages in Gaza
item.description: In a new report sent to the U.N. by Israel's health ministry, freed hostages reveal systematic abuse by Hamas, as 99 remain in captivity enduring brutal physical and psychological torment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The historic importance of Greenland for US national security as debate over island's future roars on
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's recent comments on Greenland has put the world's largest island, a district of Denmark, to the top of many foreign policy discussions. Experts weigh in on its importance to US national security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baby girl born on packed migrant boat heading for Spain's Canary Islands
item.description: A baby girl was born on a packed migrant boat headed for Spain's Lanzarote island in the Canary Islands. Authorities said the baby and her mother were in good condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK lawmakers vote against inquiry into 'rape gang scandal' as Musk keeps up pressure
item.description: British lawmakers voted against an amendment to hold a national inquiry into the U.K. grooming scandal, amid international pressure from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US strikes underground Houthi weapons depots used to hit American ships
item.description: U.S. hit weapon storage facilities used by the Iran-backed Houthi terrorist organization to target U.S. military and merchant ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
